prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde (14 atoms) was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS backend) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Fukui indices were computed via finite-difference Mulliken partial charges (PySCF) across the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) states.

The headline result is the most electrophilic site: Atom 3 (C), f⁺ = 0.39938968. The most nucleophilic site is Atom 1 (O), f⁻ = 0.23839906. Charge conservation checks passed for both f⁺ and f⁻ (sum = 1.0000). Integrity status: trustworthy = true, status = ok.

Warnings: No engine warnings were reported. The two integrity checks (fukui_charge_conservation_f+ and fukui_charge_conservation_f-) both passed. Note that negative Fukui values on hydrogen atoms are a known artifact of Mulliken partitioning with large basis sets like def2-TZVP.

Method provenance: DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard), gas phase, Mulliken charge scheme, PySCF, chemkit CLI. Geometry from user-supplied XYZ file; not re-optimized at this level. Plot saved to: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-174043_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
